def: In water-color painting, work in, or a work done chiefly in,washes, as distinguished from that done in stipple, in body color,etc.
One who makes a business of lending money on the security ofpersonal property pledged or deposited in his keeping.
Having no wings, as some insects.
In any animal, esp. of the Herbivora, a rudimentary claw orsmall hoof not reaching the ground.Some cut off the dewclaws [of greyhounds]. J. H. Walsh.
The process of attachment by garnishment. [U. S.]
Situated between or within the tropics. J. Morse.

One of the transverse plants found in the calicles of certaincorals and hydroids. Tabula rasa ( Etym: [L.], a smoothed tablet;hence, figuratively, the mind in its earliest state, before receivingimpressions from without; -- a term used by Hobbes, Locke, andothers, in maintaining a theory opposed to the doctrine of innateideas.
The male of any species of cattle (Bovidæ); hence, the male ofany large quadruped, as the elephant; also, the male of the whale.
The common title of honor in Persia, prefixed to the surname ofan individual. When appended to the surname, it signifies Prince.
